title: It's Time to Build
body: Marc Andreessen essay, April 2020
Arguments Western institutions failed during coronavirus pandemic due to inability to build

title: Overview
body: Pandemic supply failures revealed systemic building deficit
Root causes: desire, regulatory capture, political will — not money
Failures span healthcare, housing, education, manufacturing, infrastructure
Bipartisan call to action

title: Healthcare Supply Crisis
body: Insufficient tests during coronavirus pandemic
Insufficient ventilators during coronavirus pandemic
Insufficient ICU beds during coronavirus pandemic

title: Protective Equipment Shortage
body: Insufficient masks during coronavirus pandemic
Insufficient gowns during coronavirus pandemic
New York City requested rain ponchos as protective equipment

title: Not a Money Problem
body: Congress passed a $2 trillion relief package in two weeks
The problem is not money
Demonstrates rapid financial mobilization capability when desired

title: Root Causes of Building Failure
body: The problem is desire
The problem is regulatory capture
The problem is political will

title: Housing Crisis Example
body: Critiques housing prices in San Francisco
Illustrates failure to build adequate housing supply
Regulatory and political barriers prevent construction

title: Education Capacity Crisis
body: Education capacity serves microscopic share of 4 million annual U.S. eighteen-year-olds
Education capacity serves microscopic share of 120 million globally
Massive gap between demand and institutional capacity

title: Manufacturing Offshoring
body: Critiques offshored manufacturing
Loss of domestic production capability
Vulnerability exposed during pandemic supply chain disruptions

title: Missing Infrastructure: Aviation
body: Absent infrastructure like supersonic aircraft
Technological regression in commercial aviation speed
Failure to advance beyond 1970s capabilities

title: Missing Infrastructure: Logistics
body: Absent infrastructure like delivery drones
Potential efficiency gains unrealized
Regulatory barriers to deployment

title: Missing Infrastructure: Rail
body: Absent infrastructure like high-speed trains
United States lags behind international standards
Political and regulatory obstacles to modernization

title: Bipartisan Building Imperative
body: Both political sides should prioritize building
Transcends partisan divisions
National competitiveness and resilience depend on construction capacity

title: Call to Action
body: Overcome desire deficit to build
Address regulatory capture blocking construction
Generate political will for infrastructure, healthcare, education, manufacturing
Money available when priorities aligned — demonstrated by $2 trillion relief package in two weeks
